mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day horizons, or forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of co-occurring periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity that limits corporate profit margins, causing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a rising unemployment rate creates stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 5-day forward returns in greater than 65 percent of occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure exceeding 1.5 million units signals strong construction demand and wealth effects that boost consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of triggered months, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production increases corporate output and inventory turnover, directly supporting earnings growth and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth during a late-cycle expansion indicates an overheating labor market that triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Strong payroll growth in late-cycle periods yields positive 5-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in regime-specific backtests.
